audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for foot washing, Judas, Peter, cleansing/participation, example of service, beloved disciple, morsel, Satan/adversarial entry, new commandment, Peter denial.

verses.restored: Before the feast of Passover, Jesus, knowing that his hour had come to pass from this world to the Father, having loved his own in the world, loved them to the end/completion.
During supper, when the adversarial impulse had already put into the heart of Judas son of Simon Iscariot to hand him over,
Jesus, knowing that the Father had given all things into his hands, and that he came from the Divine and was going to the Divine,
rises from supper, lays aside his garments, and taking a towel, tied it around himself.
Then he pours water into the basin and began to wash the disciples' feet and wipe them with the towel tied around him.
He comes therefore to Simon Peter. He says to him, Lord, do you wash my feet?
Jesus answered and said to him, What I am doing you do not know now, but you will know after these things.
Peter says to him, You will certainly never wash my feet into the age. Jesus answered him, Unless I wash you, you have no share with me.
Simon Peter says to him, Lord, not my feet only, but also the hands and the head.
Jesus says to him, The one bathed has no need except to wash the feet, but is wholly clean; and you are clean, but not all.
For he knew the one handing him over; because of this he said, Not all of you are clean.
When therefore he had washed their feet, taken his garments, and reclined again, he said to them, Do you know what I have done to you?
You call me Teacher and Lord, and you speak beautifully/well, for I am.
If I then, the Lord and Teacher, washed your feet, you also ought to wash one another's feet.
For I gave you an example, so that just as I did to you, you also may do.
Amen, amen, I say to you: a servant is not greater than the lord, nor a sent one greater than the one who sent him.
If you know these things, blessed/flourishing are you if you do them.
I do not speak concerning all of you; I know whom I chose. But so that the Scripture may be fulfilled: The one eating my bread lifted his heel against me.
From now I tell you before it happens, so that when it happens you may trust that I am.
Amen, amen, I say to you: the one receiving whomever I send receives me, and the one receiving me receives the One who sent me.
Having said these things, Jesus was troubled in spirit and bore witness, saying, Amen, amen, I say to you that one of you will hand me over.
The disciples were looking at one another, perplexed about whom he spoke.
One of his disciples, whom Jesus loved, was reclining close to the chest of Jesus.
Simon Peter therefore gestures to this one to ask who it might be about whom he speaks.
Leaning back thus upon the chest of Jesus, he says to him, Lord, who is it?
Jesus answers, It is that one for whom I will dip the morsel and give it to him. Dipping the morsel, he takes and gives it to Judas son of Simon Iscariot.
After the morsel, the adversarial force entered into that one. Jesus therefore says to him, What you do, do quickly.
No one reclining knew for what purpose he said this to him.
Some thought, since Judas had the money box, that Jesus was saying, Buy what we need for the feast, or that he should give something to the poor.
Receiving the morsel, that one went out immediately. And it was night.
When therefore he had gone out, Jesus says, Now the Son of Humanity has been glorified, and the Divine has been glorified in him.
If the Divine has been glorified in him, the Divine will also glorify him in himself, and will glorify him immediately.
Little children, yet a little while I am with you. You will seek me; and just as I said to the Judeans, Where I go you are not able to come, I now say also to you.
A new commandment I give to you: that you love one another; just as I loved you, so you also love one another.
By this all will know that you are my disciples, if you have love among one another.
Simon Peter says to him, Lord, where are you going? Jesus answered, Where I go you are not able to follow me now, but you will follow later.
Peter says to him, Lord, why am I not able to follow you now? I will lay down my life/soul for you.
Jesus answers, Will you lay down your life/soul for me? Amen, amen, I say to you, a rooster will certainly not sound until you deny me three times.
